title: myrsine wawrae
intro: Myrsine wawrae, also known as Madeira laurel or Wawra's bay, is a shrub-like plant native to the Madeira Islands and the Canary Islands. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including relieving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and providing antioxidant properties that may help protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of Myrsine wawrae typically involve infusions or decoctions of the leaves, which can be used to make teas or applied topically in the form of creams or ointments.
